## [12:09 - 15:12]

When they hear of the success they're having with something, it helps to get that as your content that you're going to use. A lot of times when people are asked to do interviews, just the asking appeals to the ego where people like being noticed. That being said, there's plenty of people that will say no, but you'll be surprised how many yeses you'll actually get just by asking. ## [30:22 - 33:23]

if they feel safe enough with you, then they should feel safe enough hitting reply. And no, you're not going to get the social proof of everybody seeing everybody's comments in this particular case. So without using their names, you know, without calling them out, you could take those real answers and turn them into, hey, this is what everybody's been talking about here is how we handle these situations. Because the thing is this list, this 40,000 probably hasn't been emailed in three years. So that's a lot further than that. So here's what you need to do. Now there's different, there's different requirements, right? Some of them want to make sure that they double opt in so you can upload the list, but they're going to send an email out to them, asking them to, you know, reconfirm that, that you sent them the information and it's okay to be on that list. There are other email hosts that won't do that. They're just, they'll just ask you a bunch of questions and you'll tell them, yes, these people came to me via, you know, this website and they opted in during this time and, you know, these are my folks. And some of them might come back to you and ask you for more verification. Other ones will say, no, it's fine to go through with it. Either way, whatever they, you know, get uploaded into your email hosting account, you're still going to need to send out this, I apologize, email because you just have not spoken with them.

Anything less than that for me, it can be done. I mean, I've seen people test offers with 100 people, 50 people and if the offer works, the offer is going to work. So what I tell most people when they're starting out in the beginning to not spend time on building a website, do I think a website's important to have? Absolutely, but not in the beginning. I think in the beginning you're all about list building. To build a list, you don't need a website. You need a squeeze page, which you can get from lead pages, and an email hosting account. That's all you need to get started. I think once you build a list of 300, you test an offer, people are buying that offer, you're starting to build your brand. Then it makes sense to have a website. Then people want to go to a website to get more information about you. They want to start growing your business, but to get started, it's just silly to put the effort into just building a website from the beginning. In fact, more times than not, I have people telling me how long it's been taking them to build their website so they haven't had time to build their list or get an offer out to anybody. So this is the, I think this is the fifth call that we've had for Product Launch Formula since we closed the doors on the launch. So you can think of the numbers, if you start it off with 30, by the time you get to your fifth call, you're not going to have that many people on the call. When they go to like a squeeze page, the sale on that page is to get them to put in their email address and get the opt-in. So everything on that squeeze page needs to be selling why they should give you their email address. Every piece of content that you put out there that you're asking your audience to do something is really a sale, and you have to focus on each piece of content on what that sale is for that content.
